title: Velvet Hour Sessions live-set footage review: multi-clip raw-concert audit, best-take selection + unusable-clip cull, delivered as a shot-selection log with a flagged-frame contact-sheet PDF
one_line_ask: Review every raw concert clip from the Velvet Hour Sessions multi-cam shoot, judge keepers vs unusable takes, and deliver a structured shot-selection log (per-clip verdict, reason, best usable stretch, usability rating, coverage tag) plus a contact-sheet PDF of the flagged best-take frames.

assets.name: velvet_hour_raw_clips
assets.provider: seedance/seedance-1.0-pro (3 base angles) + ffmpeg (per-clip derivation, planted defects, native-audio mux)
assets.audio: native-style diegetic camera audio muxed per clip (acoustic-guitar performance bed; tuning/room-tone on pre-set + dead-air clips) via fal stable-audio; AAC 44.1kHz stereo. No music bed added in edit.

clips.defect: DEAD AIR ~24s, song only in last ~6s
DRIFTS off performer onto floor/empty chair for the second half

assets.note: QC+fix rebuild (2026-07-08): prior asset was a single ~5s SILENT clip and did NOT match the spec (folder of ~12 raw clips with native audio + planted keep/cut defects). Rebuilt as a folder of 12 raw ungraded multi-cam clips (wide/handheld/closeup) matching shoot_notes.csv 1:1, each with native-style camera audio, and the six planted defects (soft focus, camera bump, dead air, redundant take, drift-to-floor) restored so the review pass has real KEEP/MAYBE/REJECT teeth. Plain unbranded gear (blank guitar headstock, no real logos), flat camera-original look, no on-screen text.

qc_summary.qc_fix_2026_07_08: brand_note.pdf converted from markdown-renamed-.pdf to a genuine 2-page PDF (content preserved); velvet_hour_raw_clips rebuilt from a single silent 5s clip into a 12-clip folder with native audio + restored planted defects.
